mcqs.question: In a single-processor system, which of the following is true?

options.text: There are multiple general-purpose CPUs
There is one main CPU capable of executing a general-purpose instruction set
Special-purpose processors can replace the main CPU
No special-purpose processors exist

mcqs.explanation: A single-processor system has one main general-purpose CPU. It may have special-purpose processors (like disk controllers) but these cannot execute user processes.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT an advantage of multiprocessor systems?

options.text: Increased throughput
Economy of scale
Simpler operating system design
Increased reliability

mcqs.explanation: Multiprocessor systems actually require more complex OS design to handle synchronization, scheduling across CPUs, and shared memory management. The three main advantages are throughput, economy of scale, and reliability.

mcqs.question: In Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP), which statement is correct?

options.text: A master processor assigns tasks to slave processors
All processors are peers and each runs a copy of the OS
Only one processor can access memory at a time
Processors must run different operating systems

mcqs.explanation: In SMP, all processors are peers with no master-slave relationship. Each processor runs a copy of the operating system and they share a common memory. This contrasts with AMP where one master assigns tasks.

mcqs.question: What is graceful degradation in the context of multiprocessor systems?

options.text: Gradual reduction in processing speed over time
The ability to continue service proportional to surviving hardware after a failure
Automatic shutdown when a processor fails
The process of decommissioning old processors

mcqs.explanation: Graceful degradation means the system can continue providing service at a reduced level when hardware fails, rather than stopping entirely. The surviving hardware takes over the failed component's duties.

mcqs.question: In asymmetric clustering, the hot-standby node:

options.text: Runs applications and monitors other nodes
Only monitors the active server and takes over on failure
Performs parallel computation with the active node
Acts as a shared storage server

mcqs.explanation: In asymmetric clustering, one machine runs applications while the other is in hot-standby mode, only monitoring. If the active server fails, the hot-standby takes over. This wastes the standby's processing power.

mcqs.question: Which clustering approach is more efficient in using hardware resources?

options.text: Asymmetric clustering
Symmetric clustering
Both are equally efficient
Neither uses resources efficiently

mcqs.explanation: Symmetric clustering is more efficient because all nodes run applications and monitor each other simultaneously, making use of all available hardware. In asymmetric clustering, the standby node is idle during normal operation.

mcqs.question: Multicore processors are preferred over multiple single-core chips primarily because:

options.text: They have larger cache sizes
On-chip communication is faster and uses less power
They can run more operating systems
They do not require special OS support

mcqs.explanation: Multicore processors are preferred because on-chip communication between cores is faster and consumes less power than communication between separate chips. This leads to better performance per watt.

mcqs.question: A tightly coupled multiprocessor system is characterized by:

options.text: Processors connected via a network
Processors sharing memory and a common bus
Each processor having its own independent memory
Processors running different operating systems

mcqs.explanation: Tightly coupled systems (multiprocessor systems) have processors that share the computer bus, clock, memory, and peripheral devices. This enables fast inter-processor communication through shared memory.
